title: Level 0.8 (Joke)
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 0.8: "Overload of Overuse"A classification of Undetermined to excuse lacking descriptions.}} __NOTOC__ {{Joke}}{{Warningbox|color=#FFFFFF|content=This is part of the [[Hackneyverse (Joke)|Hackneyverse canon]], a series attempting to fall foul of every overused concept in the [[Backrooms Wiki:Overused Concepts Guide|Overused Concepts Guide]].}} {{Class Undetermined}} '''Level 0.8''', also known as "'''Overload of Overuse'''", is a sublevel of [[Level 0]]Its parent level having several other sublevels, if the page is a sublevel. resembling its parent level in almost all aspects..."Level X is the X+1th level in the Backrooms.", or "Level X, also known as (level nickname) resembles..."Only one minor difference when compared to their parent level, if it is a sublevel.
Oh, please... You know the [[The Database|Database]] lies to you.An unnecessarily cryptic narrator telling you the bare minimum about the level, and the bare minimum about themselves, so you don't actually have to think of lore.
|-|Zone 1: "The... Lobby?"= = Level 0.8: "The Lobby Lie" = {{Class Custom Image|color=#75623e|security=Unstable|entity=Devoid of Entities}} {{Blurimage|file=Thebackrooms.jpg|caption=Endless yellow halls...}} == Description == '''Level 0.8''', also known as '''"The Lie"''', is an expansive non-Euclidean spaceNon-Euclidean geometry. resembling the back rooms of a dated retail outlet. The level is known most prominently for its disorienting layout and distinct yellowed wallpaper. All rooms in '''Level 0.8''' share the same superficial features, with worn, moist carpeting, scattered electrical outlets, and inconsistently placed fluorescent lighting. Aside from these qualities, no two rooms in the level are the same, with each area differing significantly in shape and layout...
But that's not what you came here for, is it?
The very nature of '''Level 0.8''' is deceptive — by posing as its safe parent level, this sublevel conceals the true danger within.Manipulative features tricking the wanderer into thinking that the level is safe, where it is clearly not. Though it may seem completely devoid of peril at first glance, the deeper one strays into the depths of the level, the more noticeable the instability and corruption that subtly permeates it becomes.An increase in danger the further one explores a level, especially after specific "distance milestones".The main property being high instability/glitchiness. The fluorescent lights may buzz in strange patterns or shut off entirely, and glitches in the mono-yellow form of the level may occur, opening rifts to one of a great many secret areas hidden in '''the Chasm''' lying beyond the mouldy wallpaper. Beyond a certain point — estimated to be roughly 2000km from the "centre" of the '''Level 0.8''' — the glitching becomes almost unbearable, and further exploration without accidental noclippage into one of the secret zones is deemed impossible. == Entities == '''Level 0.8''' is seemingly devoid of all life as a result of certain [[Phenomenon 15|phenomena]] affecting the level. As it is thought to be impossible — or improbable — to find another wanderer, any and all reports detailing such a sighting are likely the result of visual and auditory hallucinations induced by prolonged isolation or higher instability levels in the deeper parts of the sublevel...
But are they really hallucinations? Or are they real? Ask yourself, dear {{USERNAME}}, is ''anything'' real?
== Colonies and Outposts == Due to the aforementioned [[Phenomenon 15|phenomenon]] which prevents wanderers from meeting in '''Level 0.8''', there have been no known established communities or outposts here...
And to think you'd want to establish a community here, of all things.
== Entrances and ExitsRandom exits or entrances to well-known levels. == === Entrances === '''Level 0.8''' may be entered through the following methods: * No-clipping through the ceiling in [[Level 0]]. * Breaking through the intestinal wall of a biological pipeline in [[Level 2]]. * Finding a yellow door labelled "Warning: Deadzone! Keep Out!" in [[Level 3]]. * Coming across a glitchy room in [[Level !]]. * Tripping on a wire in the subterranean halls of [[Level 6]]. === Exits === '''Level 0.8''' may be exited through the following methods: * No-clipping through the floor may lead back to [[Level 0]]. It may also lead to [[the Void]] or the [[Level 331|Inherently Red Rooms]]. * Finding and touching an anomalous blade of wheat may send one to [[Level 10]]. * No-clipping through a randomly placed child's drawing will lead one to a sublevel of [[Level 11]]. * Drinking [[Object 1|almond water]] from a puddle on the floor will lead to the interior of a false puddle from [[Level 1]]. * Entering a glitchier section of the level may send one to another zone of '''Level 0.8'''. |-|Zone 2: "Dread of... the Deep?"= = Level 7.4: "Dread of the Deep Deceit" = {{Class Deadzone}} {{Blurimage|file=1-296450a945.gif|caption=Endless stormy seas...}} == Description == '''Level 7.4''', also known as '''"Dread of the Deep"''', is known for its vast, infinite sea that stretches indefinitely. The winds on the level are rather strong, measuring about 200mph (approximately 322km/h), and are speculated to be what drives the ocean to such reckless conditions. Strangely, the level cannot undergo any form of precipitation, but many theorise it to be due to the indeterminable sublevel-wide source of illumination. The gravity on '''Level 7.4''' appears to be moderately low, as the waves can reach abnormally great heights, measuring almost a few megametres at most. Additionally, the sea never appears to be at ease, as its waves constantly rage in a destructive manner; consequently, the entirety of the level echoes with eternal noise due to the chaotic state of the ocean.Vast, dangerous, oceans. Time passes extremely slowly here compared to other levels, meaning that a mere few minutes spent in this sublevel could equate to several months beyond its confines.Time dilation. Across the stormy sea, strange and desolate objects are scattered: bits of scrap metal; flickering, damaged neon lights;Neon/vaporwave aesthetics. and even occasionally entire floating islands of what appears to be the ruins of a poorly built convenience store.Convenience or store related levels.Zones, especially where one of them consists of a void or ruins. Many have speculated that '''Level 7.4''' was once an idyllic, neon-lit paradise consisting of convenience stores where wanderers could purchase useful items, but had since been completely destroyed by the natural forces of the furious ocean, ripping apart every shred of the picturesque haven and rendering it a Deadzone...Once-habitable features that were ruined, causing the level to be reclassified as a Deadzone.
But who wants to read about all those fabricated mile-tall tidal waves and seismic hurricanes and ruined paradises? It's time you faced the truth.
Originally believed to be a sublevel of [[Level 7]] due to its vast ocean and general aquatic properties, it has since been proven that '''Level 7.4''' actually exists within '''the Chasm''' surrounding '''Level 0.8''', and is thus one of the secret zones a wanderer could no-clip to if they had explored too far in the unstable sectors of the sublevel. Additionally, the inclusion of neon aesthetics in '''Level 7.4''' may suggest a subtle connection to [[Level 399]], though this is often debated due to lack of evidence that supports this theorem. == Entities == There is only one known entity on '''Level 7.4'''. === [[Phorcys (Joke)|Phorcys]]Their own page, where short and unecessary, if the entity is level-specific. === [[Phorcys (Joke)|Phorcys]] is a ruthless sea deity residing deep under the swirling waters of '''Level 7.4'''. Ever-watching, never-sleeping, and nigh-omnipotent,Omnipotent beings. he stirs up whirlpools and maelstroms, and is believed to be the reason for the ungodly strength of both wind and waves in this sublevel, as well as the cause of the once-great paradise's destruction. Statues and idols depicting this being have been spotted amongst the rubble and debris floating in the ocean, and the common theory is that the inhabitants of the idyllic haven halted their worship of and rituals dedicated to [[Phorcys (Joke)|Phorcys]] due to his mean-spirited ways, thus inadvertently dooming their entire civilisation to the god's wrath...
Who is this god? In the ''Theogony'' old Hesiod called him the son of Pontos and Gaea, brother-husband of Ceto and father to many fearsome monsters. But here? He is simply a vengeful figure without mercy, residing in the Backrooms of all places, with no offspring nor consort nor parents to be found. What is the meaning of this?
== Colonies and Outposts == === The Evensong of the Deep === '''The Evensong of the Deep''' are a mysterious group of cultists who worship [[Phorcys (Joke)|Phorcys]]. Not much is known about them beyond the fact that they reside in an undiscovered location of '''Level 7.4'''.Unremarkable communities that are not expanded upon, bar a short overview on the page.Existence for one, simple purpose.Presence on a single level.
''I'' know where they reside. But that is not important. The most critical point is: do ''you'', dear {{USERNAME}}, know where they reside?
== Anomalies == Various anomalous events are known to take place in '''Level 7.4''', all of which may pose a substantial threat to the survival of a wanderer.Various events, especially where the level only has dangerous phenomena. {{Blurimage|file=oceanloop.gif|caption=A recording of the Aguabow on a particularly sunny day.}} === The Aguabow === '''The Aguabow''' is a very dangerous and anomalous phenomenon which causes tidal waves to loop back on themselves like a sort of solar flare or rainbow, hence its name. During this event, the speed of the waves affected escalate drastically, rising to a maximum of 710mph (approximately 1142km/h) and on occasion creating a whirlpool which draws in debris and wanderers alike. Waters near to the anomaly also escalate in temperature, going way above their regular boiling point but still being kept in a liquid state due to unknown properties. Those who succumb to '''the Aguabow''' often perish due to the scalding heat of its surroundings before they are even able to reach the looping tidal wave. '''The Aguabow''' is believed to be representative of the unending wrath of [[Phorcys (Joke)|Phorcys]]. {{Blurimage|file=Zjxulpt.png|caption=A photograph of a dormant Stone Siren captured by an underwater camera.}} === Stone Sirens === '''Stone Sirens''' are a type of psuedo-entity, taking the form of unmoving stone colossi that lurk underwater, resembling unclothed faceling women regarded as similar to the sirens of Greek mythology. However, during freak thunderstorms, the '''Stone Sirens''' are known to rise from underneath the waves and gain sentience, beginning to sing with a voice simultaneously melodiously beautiful and cunningly deceptive at the same time. This singing, much like the singing of classical sirens, possesses an anomalous quality which lures the unwary towards them, eventually causing the unlucky victims to be shipwrecked on their stony forms. Interestingly, the '''Stone Sirens''' never rise at the same time as the '''the Aguabow'''. The '''Stone Sirens''' are believed to be representative of the melancholy woe of [[Phorcys (Joke)|Phorcys]]. === Hallucinations === In the context of '''Level 7.4''', '''hallucinations''' are a dangerous phenomenon which oftentimes descend upon extremely sleep-deprived or starved wanderers. {{M.E.G Notice|notice=The following section is classified.Lack of public information due to danger, either generally, or through restrictive measures by the M.E.G.|reason=It has been deemed far too dangerous to release information regarding the '''hallucinations''' of '''Level 7.4''' to the public...}}
That foolish [[M.E.G.|Major Explorer Group]], restricting and classifying information from all of you unwary wanderers. Do they not see the weight of their actions?
== Entrances and Exits == === Entrances === '''Level 7.4''' may be entered through the following methods: * Drinking water from a lake coloured ultramarine blue in [[Level 10]]. * Breaking or no-clipping through the wall in [[Level 37]]. * Finding a neon light in place of a fluorescent one in [[Level 0]] and no-clipping through it. * Entering a glitchier section of '''Level 0.8'''. === Exits === '''Level 7.4''' may be exited through the following methods: * Thinking about food during a '''hallucination''' may send one to [[Level 610]]. * Tripping on a stalagmite will send one to [[Level 8]]. * Finding a working neon sign and sacrificing it to [[Phorcys (Joke)|Phorcys]] will bring one to [[Level 399]]. * Jumping down a strange hole in the middle of the ocean which is inexplicably present for no documented reason and seemingly leads into the maw of a black abyss will lead one to [[Level Fun]]. |-|Zone 3: "...Sim City?"= = Level 11.4: "Sim Sin City" = {{Class Habitable}} {{Blurimage|file=snowy_city.jpeg|caption=Endless urban landscapes...}} == Description == Much like [[Level 11|its parent level]], '''Level 11.4''' — also known as '''"Sin City"''' — is a frigid, sprawling cityscape of monolithic buildings and winding asphalt roads blanketed in a thick layer of snow.Urban cities or residential houses. The architectural design of the structures in '''Level 11.4''' varies moderately; while they often resemble the style of urban environments in the '80s and '90s, thus evoking a sense of vague nostalgia for all who stumble upon them,Artificial nostalgia, created by using "80s"/"90s" elements, completely disregarding the average age of the audience. explorations further into the level have revealed increasingly differing designs. Further alterations appear to be correlated with distance; the farther one travels from their initial entrance point, the more varied and disorderly the level's architecture seems to become. Interestingly, certain areas of the level apparently resemble cities worldwide, with wanderers stating that such areas often are placed relative to each other as they are in [[the Frontrooms]]. This may suggest that '''Level 11.4''' is an attempt to copy out the major urban settlements of [[the Frontrooms]], though this has not yet been confirmed.Replicas of Earth, or the Frontrooms in general. Unlike [[Level 11]], however, '''Level 11.4''' is not desolate, for there are many intriguing entities to be found here that often interact with wanderers. The snowy cityscape of '''Level 11.4''' is haphazardly constructed as if by procedural generation, seemingly expanded upon with little regard for aesthetics, uniformity, or habitability. Its infrastructure is largely unstable and non-Euclidean, rendering it difficult to properly map or navigate; though everything in the level is seemingly indestructible by regular means.The objects/elements of the level being indestructable. Attempts to explore the level in a linear fashion have come up short on account of its confusing architecture, with many wanderers reporting intense feelings of disorientation in their attempts to do so. The air is so unadulteratedly cold that it can cause hypothermia if one does not find a source of warmth in time,Overly high or low temperatures. however this is not a concern as many wanderers already living in the level have set up such devices as fireplaces and campfires to keep newcomers warm...
Snowstorms and fireplaces are all very well... but have you considered the sheer randomness involved in this place? Indeed, it is far more random than aught you shall ever see in the Backrooms. Something else is at play, something... higher.
Originally believed to be a sublevel of [[Level 11]] due to its sprawling cityscape and urban properties, it has since been proven that '''Level 11.4''' actually exists within '''the Chasm''' surrounding '''Level 0.8''', and is thus one of the secret zones a wanderer could no-clip to if they had explored too far in the unstable sectors of the sublevel. Researchers are currently formulating various hypotheses regarding the resemblance between '''Level 11.4''' and the [[Level 11|Concrete Jungle]], the leading of which proposes that they were separated via a [[Phenomenon 100|split]]. == GamemodesVideogame-like elements. == Upon entering the level, one of four gamemodes will be chosen for a wanderer, seemingly via a random number generator. The gamemodes and their different functions are listed below. === God Mode === This is the most anomalous and powerful gamemode of them all, granting wanderers nigh-immortality and an ability to conjure or delete anything they so wish in and out of existence, among other godly powers. '''God Mode''' is rarely bestowed, only being granted to wanderers one hundredth of the time, although — unlike the other modes — the divine powers a wanderer gains carry over to other levels as well...
And now, with a snap of the cosmic fingers, any regular man can now become a god, a divinity, a being capable of moulding the fabric of reality itself. ''Sigh...'' This is what the world has come to, I suppose.
=== Daemon Mode === '''Daemon Mode''' is similar to '''God Mode''', allowing wanderers abilities such as flight, manifestation, teleportation and invisibility, although certain aspects — such as invulnerability, as well as immunity to disease and poison, are inherently absent from this gamemode. Additionally, the powers bestowed by this mode do not carry over to other levels. However, wanderers in this gamemode are known to behave more aggressively than those in '''God''' or '''Anthropoid Mode'''. '''Daemon Mode''' is granted to around 8 out of every 100 wanderers who enter '''Level 11.4'''. === Anthropoid Mode === '''Anthropoid Mode''' is the most common gamemode, with around 82 out of every 100 wanderers having this mode befall them. In this gamemode, nothing major alters about the wanderer's biology, behaviour or abilities; thus it is considered a sort of "default" amongst those who roam the snowy landscapes of '''Level 11.4'''. This is not to say nothing at all is altered — one change upon a wanderer's body brought about by '''Anthropoid Mode''' is the formation of infinitely deep pockets in their biology, allowing them to keep a nigh-infinite number of [[Object List|objects]] in store. The changes bestowed by this gamemode do not carry over to other levels. === Spectre Mode === '''Spectre Mode''' is a gamemode which is bestowed to around 9 out of every 100 wanderers, making it ever so slightly more common than '''Daemon Mode'''. This mode casts extremely drastic alterations upon a wanderer's body, causing it to become incorporeal and nothing more than a bundle of psychic energy likened to a [[Phenomenon 9|tulpa]] in its early stages of gestation. This causes the wanderer to become intangible and unable to communicate with others (including other wanderers in '''Spectre Mode'''), although they do gain an ability to possess the minds of wanderers in corporeal gamemodes, taking control of their neurological functions and forcing them to do as their spectral invader wishes. The changes bestowed by '''Spectre Mode''' do not carry over to other levels. == Entities == In the daytime, the only entities that emerge are '''Steves'''. However, in the night, a multitude of hostile entities arise, notably [[Entity 3|Smilers]], [[Entity 2|Windows]], [[Entity 5|Clumps]], [[Entity 8|Hounds]], hostile [[Entity 9|Facelings]], [[Entity 4|Deathmoths]], [[Phenomenon 35|Wretches]], [[Sanguine Festivus|Partygoers]], [[Entity 39|Mimicries]], [[Entity 64|Cells]] and the like.Night always being more dangerous than day.The only danger being an overabundance of generic entities. === Steves === '''Steves''' are a level-specific entity resembling humans in almost all aspects, albeit being apparently trapped in '''God Mode''' eternally. They are also extremely friendly, not even attacking upon provocation, and will happily trade many strange and anomalous objects to wanderers in exchange for a regular trinket or two. Friendly beings, that tend to be overpowered. Remaining near to a '''Steve''' is shown to, inexplicably, have very beneficial impacts on the mental health of a wanderer.Positive/negative effects if one remains nearby an entity/object, or in a level. Objects that '''Steves''' can trade are often found nowhere else in the Backrooms,Extreme rarity/regularity. and may include: * '''Almond Pain.'''Almonds in some shape or form. A blend of [[Object 1|almond water]] and [[Object 12|Liquid Pain]], often causing instant death after consumption due to the anomalous properties of the almonds binding ionically with volatile chemicals in the [[Object 12|Liquid Pain]].It being found in a liquid state.It being dangerous if touched, inhaled, or eaten. * '''Bigsaws.'''It being an object from the Frontrooms, but it's extremely dangerous/beneficial. A type of jigsaw puzzle with extremely serrated edges, making for an excellent weapon against the entities of the night, as it is apparently even able to cut through the fabric of reality itself.Overpowered aspects, especially if few instances of the item exists. However, '''Bigsaws''' are notoriously difficult to put together, meaning that many wanderers fortunate enough to acquire one are still unable to use it...
A wise god would not give away their spoils so wilfully. Unfortunately, you will find, most gods are not as wise as their followers may suggest.
=== HerobrinePlagiarism of creepypastas for pages. === {{POI|name=Herobrine|status=Immortal|location=Level 11.4|affiliations=[[Entity List|Hostile Entities]]|seen=Level 11.4}} '''Herobrine''', also called '''the Dark Lord''', is believed to be a corrupted '''Steve''' who, with his godlike abilities, is the reason nighttime or entities exist in '''Level 11.4''' at all. He commands many entities and manifests only as a hazy figure of smoke and ash,Shadowy figures. being nigh-omnipotent and never tiring,Being on the list purely because they have overpowered aspects. though he seems to hate lightHatred of light. — his reluctance to act during the daytime is what researchers consider the only reason that '''Level 11.4''' has not yet been enveloped in a shroud of bloodthirsty entities...
The Dark Lord rules with an iron fist from his lair somewhere beyond the furthest reaches of the Sinful City. I know him well, yet he fears me. In fact, I may be the only person alive who he still fears to this day.
== Colonies and Outpost == There is but a single organised group of human wanderers in '''Level 11.4'''. === The EPFRO === The '''Eleven Point Four Research Organisation''', also called the '''EPFRO''', is a research groupResearch groups. that aims to explore, document and research the anomalous properties of Level '''11.4'''.Similarities to the Backroom Colonists or the M.E.G. Despite their similarity to a few more famous research groups in the Backrooms, the '''EPFRO''' maintains a strong hostility towards the [[M.E.G.]], Strong alliance with/hostility towards the M.E.G. the [[Backroom Colonists]] and many others; several have attributed this to a presence of corruption amongst their members...Strong presence of corruption.
Would you believe me if I told you that the [[Groups|group]] industry is, quite literally, a blundering bundle of copycats and unoriginalities?
== Entrances and ExitsOnly having one major entrance or exit. == === Entrances === There is only one entrance to '''Level 11.4''', through entering a glitchier section of '''Level 0.8'''. === Exits === The only way to exit '''Level 11.4''' is by using one's powers to will oneself out of the level (where applicable) or seeking a '''Steve''' or '''God/Daemon Mode''' wanderer out to perform the honours for one. |-|Zone 4: "...Solar System?"= = Level 123456789: "Solar Schlenter System" = {{Class Variable}} == Description == '''Level 123456789''', also known as the '''"Schlenter System"''', is what seems to be a recreation of Earth's solar system, containing nine subzones bearing symbolic semblance to the planets and Sun of [[the Frontrooms]].A very large number slot for a level, yet still with a basic concept.A recreation of Earth's solar system with various different planets. Although much remains unknown about the nature of this level except that it is indeed a zone of '''Level 0.8''' floating within '''the Chasm''', much effort has gone into procuring the little information that could be obtained to sufficiently prepare wanderers should they stumble into this level. Below is a list of the little garnered information about various subzones of '''Level 123456789'''. === Subzone 0: "Sol" === '''Subzone 0''', or '''Sol''', is a massive, radiant plain of light. It seems to be devoid of entities and, indeed, anything else, and serves as a starting point for those entering the level. It is representative of the Sun. === Subzone 1: "The Caduceus" === '''Subzone 1''', or '''the Caduceus''', takes the form of a massive statue of a caduceus erected upon a barren grey landscape. Curled around the statue are two live snakes not unlike [[the Serpent]], who devour any wanderer who dares to approach. This subzone may be inexplicably entered after 2-3 hours of roaming '''Sol''', and is exited via no-clipping through a stone seashell carved into the base of the caduceus statue. It is representative of Mercury. === Subzone 2: "Foam of Venus" === '''Subzone 2''', or the '''Foam of Venus''', is a vast, calm ocean resembling levels such as [[Level 7]]. On the surface, clouds of foam gather and massive seashells bob up and down aimlessly. This subzone is entered via no-clipping through a stone seashell carved into the base of '''the Caduceus''', and may be exited through a suspicious underwater kelp forest whose seaweed resembles pine trees. The '''Foam of Venus''' is representative of Venus. === Subzone 3: "Grove of the Gods" === '''Subzone 3''', or the '''Grove of the Gods''', is a vast forest of pines with seemingly no anomalous properties whatsoever.Vast forests, with zero or one unique properties. This subzone is entered through a suspicious underwater kelp forest whose seaweed resembles pine trees in the '''Foam of Venus''', and may be exited when the sky turns red (which happens on a 34-hour basis). This subzone is representative of Earth. === Subzone 4: "A Warrior's Lament" === '''Subzone 4''', or '''A Warrior's Lament''', is a red barren field where flecks of dead grass emerge from cracks in the ground. Dried blood may also be found, suggesting that this plain was once a battlefield. This subzone may be entered when the sky turns red in the '''Grove of the Gods''', and exited by no-clipping through the rare eagle carving that may ever and anon appear. It is representative of Mars. === Subzone 5: "Royal Match" === '''Subzone 5''', or the '''Royal Match''', is an arena in which the wanderer must fist-fight against [[Entity 9|faceling]] royalty. The subzone is entered by no-clipping through a carving of an eagle in '''A Warrior's Lament''', and may be exited if the wanderer successfully beats all three [[Entity 9|faceling]] princes and their king. The '''Royal Match''' is representative of Jupiter. === Subzone 6: "Saturnine Longing" === '''Subzone 6''', or '''Saturnine Longing''', is a long hallwayLong hallways. lit by gloomy iron lanterns bearing resemblance to those of the European Dark Ages. Wanderers who enter this subzone often go insane due to the sheer aura of depression and wanton longing permeating it.Out-of-nowhere insanity, typically anxious feelings or depressive thoughts. The subzone is entered if the wanderer successfully beats all three [[Entity 9|faceling]] princes and their king in a '''Royal Match''', and may be exited by walking to the end of the hallway and stepping through the bright blue door. It is representative of Saturn. === Subzone 7: "The Skybox" === '''Subzone 7''', or '''the Skybox''', is exactly what its name states — a bright blue sky, and nothing more. Further research is needed to determine other properties of the subzone. All that is known is that it may be entered by walking to the end of '''Saturnine Longing''' and stepping through the bright blue door, and exited by mounting a pegasus that may manifest at odd intervals. It is representative of Uranus. === Subzone 8: "Equestria" === '''Subzone 8''', or '''Equestria''', is a country of horses where the equine run the government and many other factions, whilst humans and [[Entity 9|facelings]] are put to use as slaves. Connection of this subzone to [[the Mare]] is being investigated. The subzone may be entered by mounting a pegasus that appears in '''the Skybox''' randomly, and exited by nicely asking the equine government officials to let you out of the level. It is representative of Neptune...
Ah, you really thought that was it, didn't you? Well, I'm afraid to break the news to you, but...
=== Subzone 9: "The Underworld" === '''Subzone 9''', or '''the Underworld''', is a tight series of suffocating caves that will eventually kill a wanderer due to lack of oxygen. It may be entered if the equine officials believe one unfit to return to [[Backrooms Wiki|the world beyond]] and proceed to throw one down to "the dungeon" — which, of course, refers to this subzone. It is representative of Pluto, and cannot ever be exited. == Entities == There are various entities in various subzones of '''Level 123456789''', including the gigantic amorphous serpents of '''the Caduceus'''; the [[Entity 9|faceling]] royal family of the '''Royal Matches''' who seem to be able to revive themselves after every battle; the pegasus messenger who manifests in '''the Skybox''' but presumably lives in '''Equestria'''; as well as all the other equine individuals who take up residence in '''Subzone 8'''...
Snakes, faceless men, winged horses... Get out of your lucid children's dream. It's time you faced the hard truth of reality.
== Colonies and Outposts == There are no human colonies or outposts in '''Level 123456789''', though '''Equestria''', as a country of horses, could certainly be considered as a colony...
Think long and hard... If your kind cannot even overthrow some horses, then how will you change the world? Are you any better than the other entities of this wretched realm?
== Entrances and Exits == === Entrances === '''Level 123456789''' can only be entered when one thinks for too long about the Sun on a [[Special:Random/Class Habitable|Class Habitable]] level. === Exits === '''Level 123456789''' can only be exited by making it through the eight subzones and getting permission from the horse government officials of '''Equestria''' to leave. However, if one is sent to the secret sector of '''the Underworld''', there is apparently no escape. == Addendum == {{Collapse|num=0|initial=
> Open Log <Logs that serve no benefit, information-wise.
|final=
> Close Log <
|body=
Oh dear, not this again... Yeah, as you can tell by the live recording, I'm in a radiant plane that kinda stretches on infinitely. I'll see what I can find. {{Underline|FFFFFF}} ''*two hours of silence*'' {{Underline|FFFFFF}} Woah, is that a statue of some staff or something? And are those... living snakes? Uh oh, I think my video camera's battery is dyi— ''*cuts off*''
}}
|-|Zone 5: "A Long Forgotten... Exclamation?"= = Level ? = {{Class Undetermined}} {{Blurimage|file=Hallway-at-work-v0-x7zm2op2zksa1.webp|caption=Endless enigmatic hallways...}} == Description == '''Level ?''', also known as '''"A Long Forgotten Question"''', is an anomalous, enigmatic sublevel of [[Level !]]. It manifests as a set of incredibly long hallways lit by bright fluorescent lights and with well-carpeted flooring, that intersect every few kilometres or so with almost no alterations in their appearance. In addition, doors which lead to stairwells often appear, leading to separate floors that seem to be an exact replica of the original,Floors, especially when they have no changes whatsoever and they are infinite. though it is known that [[Phenomenon 11|instability]] increases the further one gets from the "beginning" storey. Unlike [[Level !|its parent level]], '''Level ?''' is believed to be completely safe, even presenting itself as a [https://backrooms.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Safe_Haven safe haven]:Generic safe havens being literally the only unique property. on the walls, a door may ever and anon break the monotony, leading to areas like attics or basements that contain ungodly amounts of [[Object 1|almond water]], [[Object 2|level keys]], [[Object 6|moth jelly]], [[Object 10|backshrooms]], [[Object 27|Mixologist's Delight]], [[Object 17|Lubik's cubes]], [[Object 34|elevators]] and many more useful items, albeit having little to no other anomalous properties.Rooftops, attics or basements, with zero odd properties whatsoever. For this reason, wanderers often choose to spend the rest of their lives here despite the liminal atmosphere, due to the fear of the dangers which lie beyond...
Just hallways, more hallways, and even more hallways... Now this is something I can work with.
Originally believed to be a sublevel of [[Level !]] due to its multitude of long corridors and hallways, it has since been proven that '''Level ?''' actually exists within '''the Chasm''' surrounding '''Level 0.8''', and is thus one of the secret zones a wanderer could no-clip to if they had explored too far in the unstable sectors of the sublevel. However, it is by far the rarest to enter through this fashion, and wanderers are far more likely to end up in either '''Level 7.4''' or '''Level 11.4''' when stumbling into the glitchiest areas of '''Level 0.8''''s first zone. == Entities == No entities have been officially documented in '''Level ?''', but rumours of a certain attic-dwelling creature are indeed present across the inhabitants of the level. === Rooftiles === '''Rooftiles''' are, allegedly, an enigmatic entity residing only in the attic sections of '''Level ?''', posing as tiles of the roof that make up these rooms' triangular ceilings.Features that mimic inanimate objects. Being hostile entities, '''Rooftiles''' are known to sometimes drop from the ceilings when a wanderer is under them, causing them to be struck on the head with the heavy brick-eqsue material composing these entities and almost always resulting in concussions or death. In severe cases, every '''Rooftile''' in an attic has been recorded to fall simultaneously, resulting in the room's immediate destabilisation and collapse into '''the Chasm'''. They may be repelled with [[Object 1|almond water]] or any similar substance.A certainty that it can be repelled by almond water. Intriguingly, it seems that '''Rooftiles''', for whatever reason, are only hostile towards humans, as a [[Entity 9|faceling]] who had wandered '''Level ?''' has stated that he attempted to provoke the '''Rooftiles''' to check if they were truly sentient, but to no avail...Hostility only directed at humans for no reason.
These deceptive little devils may be more of an allegory than one may realise... After all, is the [[The Database|Database]] not just as deceitful?
== Colonies and Outposts == Due to the sheer size of the level, it is seemingly impossible for more than three wanderers to meet up at a time in '''Level ?'''. Coupling this with the [[Phenomenon 14|non-Euclidean nature]] of the sublevel, the creation of colonies has been deemed fully and utterly implausible...
You foolish humans, always seeking to ruin the perfect essence of liminality with your stubborn will to band together...
== Entrances and Exits == === Entrances === '''Level ?''' may be entered through the following methods: * Walking through a door branded with a question mark in [[Level 330]]. * Finding the hallway of [[Level !]] inexplicably fading into this level around oneself. * No-clipping through a car in [[Level 12]]. * Walking down an extremely long hallway in [[Level 4]], which acts as a seamless transition. * Standing under a faulty fluorescent light in [[the End]] for approximately 6 hours. * Did you no that you can get here by no-clipping through the walls of [[Level 47]]? * Entering a glitchier section of '''Level 0.8'''. === Exits === '''Level ?''' may be exited through the following methods: * Breaking through the floor of an attic or the ceiling of a basement will lead one to either [[Level 28]] or [[the Attic]]. * Rarely, no-clipping through the walls may lead one back to [[Level 47]], if that was the level one originally entered from, can't it? * Traversing into an exceptionally unstable location may no-clip one into [[the Void]], '''the Chasm''' or even [[the Broken]]. * Traversing extremely far up the floors without accidentally no-clipping may send one to [[Absolute Infinity Of Unknown|the Absolute Infinity of the Unknown]]. * Traversing extremely far down the floors without accidentally no-clipping may send one to [[Level REDACTED|the Downrooms]]. |-|Zone 6: "The... Void?"= = The Chasm = {{Class 0|class=Limbo|hexagon=✠|safety=Unpredictable|security=Semi-Existent|entity=Between Worlds|banner-text=white|color-l=10%|}} == Description == '''The Chasm''' is a sublevel of [[the Void]], resembling its parent level in almost all aspects.Voids. Very little is known about this space, though it is known that the location is an ephemeral, transitory space temporarily accessed during the process of no-clipping. It is speculated to be the space "in-between" the four other subsectors of '''Level 0.8''', acting as a nexus through which each may be accessed. It appears to be boundless in scale, lacking any visible spatial borders. It is completely lightless and empty, lacking any visual or auditory indicators of one's location in its space. One is completely isolated and alone in '''the Chasm'''; it is currently unknown if it is statistically improbable to encounter other wanderers within due to its sheer scale, or if every being experiences their own — identically empty — reality... Various theories about its separateness from [[the Void]] have been formulated; some even speculate that '''the Chasm''' may be the result of an ancient [[Phenomenon 100|split]] that left a portion of [[the Void]] simply floating amongst [[Uncharted Territory]] — doomed to an eternity of ceaseless, stagnant dilapidationThe sublevel being the dilapidated version of its parent level, if it is a sublevel. while its parent abyss continued churning on and on without ever so much as a second thought... == Entities == There are no documented entities in '''the Chasm'''... == Colonies and Outposts == There are no colonies and outposts in '''the Chasm'''... == Entrances and Exits == === Entrances === Accessing a particularly unstable section of any of '''Level 0.8''''s zones is likely to send one here... === Exits === A wanderer is usually automatically transported out of '''the Chasm''' a few minutes after they first access it, being led to another sector of '''Level 0.8'''. However, there have been cases where certain individuals have been trapped in limbo for a long period of time, though these are rare...
Are you wondering why I've been so quiet? {{USERNAME}}, I think it's time you knew a little something about me. [[The Onyx Archbishop (Joke)|Let me tell you who I am.]]
